able to incise the fascia and this was achieved by dissecting thickened omental adhesions as well as colonic adhesions from the
midline, and then incising fascia in a serial manner.
Once the midline was cleared and the fascia had been opened, I was then
was then reflected cephalad, Bookwalter retractor was placed.
There were 2 areas of inadvertent serosal injury, secondary to very dense adhesions, and these
were repaired using sutures of 3-0 silk in Lembert fashion.
The left colon was then mobilized along the line of Toldt, colonic mesentery and
retroperitoneum were separated along the avascular plane, the sigmoid was noted to be very inflamed, thickened.
the sigmoid away from the pelvis by using sharp dissection, as it was very densely adherent to this area.

surroundings, and I had to further finger fracture this area away from its pelvic attachments.
Once this was completed, I was
able to reach the soft portion of the rectum, which was in the midportion.
A window was created in the mesentery of
the descending colon, and the colon divided using a contour stapler and a green load.
The mesentery was then taken down to
the proximal rectum using Ligasure, some of the mesorectum was divided in order for us to get down to the mid rectum for
Contour stapler was then used to divide the mid rectum, using a green load.

Bleeding from the sigmoid mesentery was oversewn using 2-0 silk in figure-of-eight fashion.
The rectal stump was tagged with a 2-0 Prolene.
The left colon was then mobilized further along the white line of Toldt, separating mesentery from retroperitoneum along the
avascular plane, up to the level of the splenic flexure.
The splenic flexure was mobilized from attachments using sharp and blunt dissection.
was opened, and this allowed me to fully mobilize the splenic flexure.

subcutaneous tissue away from the anterior fascia in all directions.

out a right lower quadrant stab incision.
The drain was secured using nylon.
There was still some hernia sac present and this was sharply excised free down to the level of the fascia and discarded.

chartSnapshot.primary_reason_for_home_health_medical_n: The patient is considered homebound because she is recovering from a recent complex open lower anterior resection with end colostomy, incisional hernia repair, and bilateral myocutaneous flaps. She presents with an open midline abdominal wound, approximately 3cm in length, which requires skilled wound care including packing changes. This medical condition and the associated post-operative recovery severely limit her ability to leave her residence.
chartSnapshot.homebound_narrative: The patient is recovering from a recent complex open lower anterior resection with end colostomy, incisional hernia repair, and bilateral myocutaneous flaps. She has an open midline abdominal wound, approximately 3cm in length, which requires skilled wound care including packing changes. This medical condition and the associated post-operative recovery severely limit her ability to leave her residence.
chartSnapshot.homebound_supporting_factors: Severe pain on ambulation, Exhausting to navigate vehicle and require human assist, Requires assist to manage equipment and public transportation
